_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, consisting of an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 = 100, currently stands at 132, about 32% higher than its 2015 level. This increase reflects a notable rise in commodity prices over the period. The climb has been uneven across the items, with some showing substantial gains while others exhibit only modest changes. The overall basket movement is therefore the result of diverging trends within the component commodities.
notes.movers: Coffee and orange juice have been the primary drivers, with coffee prices up 93% and orange juice up 22% since 2015. Wheat and sugar prices increased more moderately, by 7% and 5% respectively. These changes largely arise from supply disruptions driven by weather events and crop diseases—citrus greening and hurricanes for orange juice, and frost or drought in arabica coffee growing regions. Given that these are soft commodities traded in relatively thin markets, such factors can cause sharp price movements disconnected from broad inflation trends.
notes.why: The headline increase in the breakfast basket is substantially influenced by a few weather-sensitive crops. As such, prices are vulnerable to episodic shocks that may keep input costs elevated over extended periods. This underscores the importance of climate and disease impacts in shaping global soft commodity price dynamics.
